ranked_movies.title: Fight Club
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively analyzed and discussed for its deep themes of masculinity, consumerism, and finding balance, making it the most critically examined film across all subreddits.

ranked_movies.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended as both a feel-good comfort film and a visually stunning masterpiece, appearing in multiple threads.

ranked_movies.title: The Princess Bride
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a classic warm blanket comfort film that is endlessly rewatchable and leaves viewers smiling.

ranked_movies.title: Swiss Army Man
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as an extremely weird, thought-provoking film that also serves as a surprisingly feel-good experience with a unique ending.

ranked_movies.title: Almost Famous
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a cozy, comforting film that feels like a warm blanket and is perfect for endless rewatches.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a top-tier comfort film that is hilarious, heartwarming, and highly rewatchable.

ranked_movies.title: Secretary
ranked_movies.reason: Praised as an underrated hidden gem with depth, strong character development, and nuanced storytelling.

ranked_movies.title: Moon
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a shockingly underrated film with a standout performance by Sam Rockwell and deep, thoughtful storytelling.

ranked_movies.title: Being John Malkovich
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as an extremely weird and psychological film that makes viewers think 'WTF' due to its surreal and unique premise.

ranked_movies.title: Gladiator
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film where the villain's death is intensely satisfying, contributing to its iconic status.

ranked_movies.title: Walk Hard: The Dewey Cox Story
ranked_movies.reason: Celebrated as one of the best comedy films, with John C. Reilly's performance being particularly praised.

ranked_movies.title: My Cousin Vinny
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a feel-good film with a funny and earned happy ending that leaves viewers smiling.

ranked_movies.title: Boiling Point
ranked_movies.reason: Critically analyzed for its unique directing style, thematic depth, and as a standout film in Kitano's filmography.

ranked_movies.title: Shawshank Redemption
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a film that leaves viewers feeling good and smiling by the credits due to its hopeful and uplifting ending.

ranked_movies.title: Inglourious Basterds
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film where the bad guys' deaths are particularly satisfying and cathartic.

ranked_movies.title: Death to Smoochy
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as an underrated hidden gem with a cult following and unique storytelling.

ranked_movies.title: Eraserhead
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an extremely weird, psychological film that feels like a fever dream and leaves viewers unsettled.

ranked_movies.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its gorgeous cinematography and as an underrated film with depth and strong visual storytelling.

ranked_movies.title: Pleasantville
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a feel-good film that leaves viewers smiling and offers a thoughtful, uplifting message.

ranked_movies.title: The Departed
